title: acinos alpinus f. hirsutus
intro: Acinos alpinus f. hirsutus, also known as hairy alpine thyme, is a subspecies of the Acinos alpinus plant, a member of the mint family native to the mountainous regions of Europe and Asia. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which have been used to treat various health issues such as wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory problems. The health benefits of Acinos alpinus f. hirsutus include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate digestive issues, and exhibit antioxidant and antimicrobial properties. Herbal preparations of the plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal and health benefits.
